User.title: User
User.description: Represents an individual user of the FinTrack Automation application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for communication.

organizationIds.description: References to Organizations this user belongs to or manages. (Relationship: User N:N Organization)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Organization.title: Organization
Organization.description: Represents a business or entity whose financial data is managed by the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Organization entity.

name.description: The legal name of the organization.

description.description: A brief description of the organization.

address.description: The primary physical address of the organization.

contactEmail.description: The primary contact email for the organization.

contactPhone.description: The primary contact phone number for the organization.

currency.description: The primary operating currency for the organization (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the organization record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the organization record was last updated.

Account.title: Account
Account.description: Represents a financial account (e.g., bank, credit card, cash) belonging to an organization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Account entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization that owns this account. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Account)

name.description: A user-friendly name for the account (e.g., 'Main Checking', 'Savings', 'Business Credit Card').

type.description: The type of the financial account (e.g., 'Bank', 'Credit Card', 'Cash').

currentBalance.description: The current balance of the account.

description.description: An optional description for the account.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the account record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the account record was last updated.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this category belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Category)

type.description: The financial type of the category (e.g., 'Income', 'Expense').

description.description: An optional description for the category.

isSystemDefined.description: Indicates if the category is a default system-provided category or user-defined.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the category record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the category record was last updated.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a single financial transaction, such as an expense or income.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this transaction belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Transaction)

accountId.description: Reference to the Account where this transaction occurred. (Relationship: Account 1:N Transaction)

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category assigned to this transaction. (Relationship: Category 1:N Transaction)

documentId.description: Optional reference to the Document from which this transaction was extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:N Transaction)

amount.description: The monetary value of the transaction. Positive for income, negative for expense.

type.description: The overall type of the transaction (e.g., 'Income', 'Expense', 'Transfer').

description.description: A brief description of the transaction.

transactionDate.description: The date and time when the transaction occurred.

merchant.description: The name of the merchant or source/destination of the transaction.

isAutomated.description: Indicates if this transaction was automatically extracted and entered (e.g., via OCR).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was last updated.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Stores details about financial documents (e.g., receipts, invoices) uploaded for automated data entry.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this document belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Document)

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who uploaded this document. (Relationship: User 1:N Document)

filename.description: The original filename of the uploaded document.

fileUrl.description: The URL or path where the document file is stored.

fileType.description: The MIME type or file extension of the document (e.g., 'application/pdf', 'image/jpeg').

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the document was uploaded.

extractedText.description: The full text extracted from the document using OCR.

processingStatus.description: The current processing status of the document (e.g., 'Uploaded', 'Processing', 'Processed', 'Error').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was last updated.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a client or customer of an organization, typically for invoicing purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this customer is associated with. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Customer)

name.description: The name of the customer (e.g., company name or individual's name).

contactPerson.description: The name of the primary contact person at the customer's organization.

email.description: The primary email address for the customer.

phone.description: The primary phone number for the customer.

address.description: The billing address of the customer.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was last updated.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Manages invoices created and sent by an organization to its customers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization that created this invoice. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Invoice)

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer to whom this invoice is sent. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Invoice)

invoiceNumber.description: A unique, human-readable identifier for the invoice (e.g., 'INV-2023-001').

issueDate.description: The date when the invoice was issued.

dueDate.description: The date by which the invoice payment is due.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the invoice.

status.description: The current status of the invoice (e.g., 'Draft', 'Sent', 'Paid', 'Overdue', 'Cancelled').

paymentTerms.description: Description of the payment terms (e.g., 'Net 30', 'Due on receipt').

notes.description: Optional notes or comments for the invoice.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice record was last updated.

InvoiceItem.title: InvoiceItem
InvoiceItem.description: Represents a single line item within an invoice.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InvoiceItem entity.

invoiceId.description: Reference to the Invoice this item belongs to. (Relationship: Invoice 1:N InvoiceItem)

description.description: A description of the product or service billed.

unitPrice.description: The price per unit of the item.

total.description: The total price for this item (quantity * unitPrice).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice item record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice item record was last updated.

ReportConfiguration.title: ReportConfiguration
ReportConfiguration.description: Stores the configuration settings for a customizable financial report.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ReportConfiguration entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this report configuration belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N ReportConfiguration)

name.description: A descriptive name for the report configuration (e.g., 'Monthly Income Statement Q1').

reportType.description: The type of financial report (e.g., 'Income Statement', 'Balance Sheet', 'Cash Flow').

parameters.description: A JSON string or opaque object storing specific parameters for report generation (e.g., '{"startDate":"2023-01-01", "endDate":"2023-03-31"}').

lastGeneratedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who last generated a report using this configuration. (Relationship: User 1:N ReportConfiguration)

lastGeneratedDate.description: The timestamp when a report was last generated using this configuration.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the report configuration was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the report configuration was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the owner matching `request.auth.uid`. Includes an `organizationIds` array for efficient querying of user's associated organizations.

Root collection for organizations. Documents include a `members` map (`{[userId]: "role"}`) for collaborative access control, which is denormalized to all child documents for authorization independence.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the organization.

definition.description: Financial accounts belonging to an organization. Includes denormalized `organizationId` and `members` map for authorization independence and simplified rules.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent organization.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the account.

definition.description: Transaction categories defined for an organization. Includes denormalized `organizationId` and `members` map for authorization independence and simplified rules.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent organization.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the category.

definition.description: Individual financial transactions within an organization. Includes denormalized `organizationId` and `members` map for authorization independence and simplified rules.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent organization.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the transaction.

definition.description: Financial documents (e.g., receipts, invoices) uploaded by users for an organization. Includes denormalized `organizationId`, `uploadedByUserId`, and `members` map for authorization independence.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent organization.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the document.

definition.description: Customer records associated with an organization. Includes denormalized `organizationId` and `members` map for authorization independence and simplified rules.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent organization.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the customer.

definition.description: Invoices created by an organization. Includes denormalized `organizationId`, `customerId`, and `members` map for authorization independence and simplified rules.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent organization.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the invoice.

definition.description: Line items for a specific invoice. Includes denormalized `organizationId` (inherited from parent Invoice) and `members` map for authorization independence.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grandparent organization.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent invoice.

params.name: invoiceItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the invoice item.

definition.description: Configuration settings for customizable financial reports for an organization. Includes denormalized `organizationId`, `lastGeneratedByUserId`, and `members` map for authorization independence.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent organization.

params.name: reportConfigurationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the report configuration.

definition.description: Represents global administrators. Documents in this collection exist solely to grant 'admin' system-level roles based on the user's UID (DBAC - existence over content). No sensitive user data should be stored here.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Auth UID).

firestore.reasoning: The FinTrack Automation Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust, debuggable, and scalable security rules. The core design principle involves denormalizing authorization context, specifically the 'members' map, into documents that require access control based on organizational membership. This strategy eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, which can break atomic operations and complicate debugging.1. Authorization Independence via Denormalization: The `Organization` entity serves as the primary collaborative unit. Each `Organization` document (`/organizations/{organizationId}`) will contain a `members` map (e.g., `{"uid1":"admin", "uid2":"editor"}`). Critically, all sub-collection documents directly or indirectly owned by an organization (e.g., `Accounts`, `Categories`, `Transactions`, `Documents`, `Customers`, `Invoices`, `ReportConfigurations`, and `InvoiceItems`) will inherit and store a denormalized copy of this `members` map from their respective parent `Organization`. This allows security rules to check `resource.data.members[request.auth.uid]` directly within the document itself, making authorization decisions atomic and independent of parent document fetches. This approach is vital for supporting efficient transactions and batch writes across related entities.2. Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture): All collections under `/organizations/{organizationId}` (e.g., `/organizations/{organizationId}/accounts`, `/organizations/{organizationId}/transactions`) are designed to have a homogeneous security posture. Every document within these collections requires a user to be a member of the specified `organizationId` with an appropriate role. This clear segregation prevents mixing data with different access requirements in the same collection, thereby simplifying security rules. Instead of complex logical checks, rules can rely on the structural context and the denormalized 'members' map present in every document.3. Path-Based Ownership and Hierarchy: User-specific private data is stored under `/users/{userId}`, ensuring direct ownership via `request.auth.uid`. Collaborative and organizational data is nested under `/organizations/{organizationId}`, clearly establishing organizational context. Further nested entities like `InvoiceItem` maintain this hierarchy (`/organizations/{organizationId}/invoices/{invoiceId}/invoiceItems/{invoiceItemId}`), ensuring that the denormalized `members` map from the `Organization` is propagated down, making security checks consistent at any level.4. QAPs (Query as Authorization Patterns): The structure is designed to enable secure `list` operations without relying on rules as filters. For a user to list their organizations, the `organizationIds` array within their `/users/{userId}` document provides a direct reference for client-side queries. For listing data within an organization (e.g., 'all transactions for Org X'), queries are inherently secure because every `Transaction` document (and any other document in an organization's subtree) contains the denormalized `members` map. This allows Firestore rules to efficiently verify `request.auth.uid` against `resource.data.members` for each document returned by the query, ensuring only authorized data is accessed without needing additional database lookups. This structure provides a clear, maintainable, and secure foundation for FinTrack Automation.
